MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD
Off Calendar Apr-30-2024 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearance via Zoom:
Kimberly Chu, Esq. of Brayton Purcell, Tel. 415.898.1555, appearing via Zoom for Plaintiff Rosemarie Bruno as Successor-in-Interest to and as Wrongful Death Heir of Patrick Kincebach, Deceased;
No appearance by any other party or counsel.

Hearing re: Brayton Purcell's Motion to be Relieved as Counsel.

The Court inquires about service. Ms. Chu states that they provided Ms. Bruno notice but they did not make contact with Ms. Bruno. Ms. Chu also stated that they provided Ms. Bruno with notice of the motion via certified and registered mail, and that as to the tentative ruling that they called and left Ms. Bruno messages.

At the conclusion of Ms. Chu's comments, the Court adopted the tentative ruling as follows:

Nancy Williams, Brayton Purcell LLP's Motion to be Relieved as Counsel for plaintiff Rosemarie Bruno is GRANTED. No opposition filed. Counsel provided proper notice and complied with the service requirements pursuant to CRC 3.1362.

The Court will review the proposed order submitted by Brayton Purcell.

Judge: Rochelle C. East. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Matter Not Reported. Bailiff: Not Present. (301/RCE)

Appearances by Court Call: Nancy Erwin Buck of Pond North (213.617.6170) for Genuine Parts Company; Robert Bugatto of Hugo Parker (415.808.0300) for O'Reilly Auto Enterprises; Edward Tugade of Demler Armstong (415.949.1900) for Pneumo Abex; No other appearances.

The Court did not receive notification that the tentative ruling is opposed. This matter is not argued. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: GRANTED under C.C.P. section 473(a)(1).
